transcript: In, uh, in Eastern thought, the notion of time is that of a cycle, not it's not linear, time is cyclical. And therefore, the climax in our stories arrives in the middle of the story, not at the end. The Gita is no different. Just when you reach about chapter 10, there are 18 chapters in all, and the philosophy begins to get a little overwhelming, a new twist is introduced into the tale. We are told that Krishna is not who we have believed all along. In chapter 11, it is followed by drama and spectacle with Krishna revealing his cosmic Vishwaroopa form. Our attentions have been recaptured and we remain vested for the next 7 chapters. If you were looking for a guide on how to write a riveting bestseller, you need to look no further than the Gita.
